steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined sentence is the last one: "Such analysis, in turn, can contribute to reports of exaggerated effects." The phrase 'in turn' signals a consequence flowing from what came before: biased analysis (caused by small sample sizes) leads to exaggerated reports.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It summarizes a shift in scientists' understanding of how *Hippocampus guttulatus* has responded to ocean acidification.: no shift in understanding is described; the sentence adds a downstream consequence. ✗
  • Choice B — It elaborates on a potential consequence of Faleiro and colleagues' reliance on a relatively small sample size.: traces the causal chain from small sample size to biased analysis to exaggerated reports. ✓
  • Choice C — It emphasizes the magnitude of the effect reported by Faleiro and colleagues of ocean acidification on *Hippocampus guttulatus*.: the sentence warns about exaggeration, it does not amplify Faleiro's claim. ✗
  • Choice D — It counters the objection of Clements and colleagues to studies that rely on relatively small sample sizes.: the sentence supports Clements' concern, not counters it. ✗
That matches Choice B — It elaborates on a potential consequence of Faleiro and colleagues' reliance on a relatively small sample size..
